welcome.headline: Let's set up your workspace
welcome.subtitle: A quick, one-time setup. Point Hyperion to Cyberpunk 2077 and choose where your mods live — it takes less than a minute.

game.label: Game
game.heading: Where is Cyberpunk 2077 installed?
game.description: Point Hyperion to your game folder. We use it to verify the install and deploy your mods safely.

library.label: Mod library
library.heading: Where should your mods live?
library.description: Every mod you add is kept here — organized, staged, and ready to enable whenever you want.
library.preview: Where your managed mods are stored

downloads.label: Downloads
downloads.heading: Where do new downloads land?
downloads.description: Hyperion picks up new mod archives from this folder. We've suggested one next to your library — change it anytime.
downloads.preview: Where new mod archives are picked up

nexus.label: Nexus
nexus.heading: Connect your Nexus account
nexus.description: Paste your personal Nexus Mods API key to enable downloads and update checks. You can skip this and add it later in Settings > Nexus.

game.placeholder: No folder selected — detect or browse below
game.valid: Cyberpunk 2077 found — you're good to go.
game.invalid: We couldn't find Cyberpunk 2077 in this folder.
game.continueTooltip: Select a valid Cyberpunk 2077 folder to continue

library.placeholder: No folder selected — use the suggestion or browse
library.valid: This folder is ready to use.
library.invalid: This folder can't be used as a mod library.
library.continueTooltip: Select a valid mod library folder to continue

downloads.info: New mod downloads will be saved here.

nexus.instruction1: Log in to Nexus Mods and open the API Key Settings page.
nexus.instruction2: Scroll to the bottom and find Personal API Key.
nexus.instruction3: Copy your personal API key and paste it below.
nexus.apiKeyPlaceholder: Paste your Nexus Mods API key
nexus.optionalInfo: Optional — you can add this later in Settings > Nexus.
nexus.error: We couldn't validate this key. Double-check you copied the Personal API Key.

welcome.finishTooltip: Select a valid game folder and mod library to finish

toast.finishInvalid: Select a valid game folder and mod library before finishing
toast.purged: Purged {count} active mod(s) from the previous deployment
toast.purgeFailed: Could not fully purge {count} mod(s) from the previous deployment
toast.restorePartial: Loaded library, but {count} active mod(s) could not be restored

toolbar.title: Managed Mods
toolbar.titleTooltip:
Managed Mods: list of mods managed by the Hyperion library.
Use the 'Install Mod' button to add a mod. Use 'Reinstall' to reinstall from the original source file.
toolbar.quickSelectTooltip:
QUICK SELECTION:
Click in a mod and then select another mod while holding shift to select multiple mods.
Ctrl+A to select all mods.
toolbar.customOrderTooltip:
Custom Order active.
Drag a mod onto a separator to group it.
Drag before/after a row to reorder.
Drag to the header to move a mod to top-level.
toolbar.checkUpdatesTooltip: Check installed Nexus mods for newer versions
toolbar.deleteAllTooltip: Delete all mods from the current library

header.bulkUnavailableEnabled: Unavailable while Enabled filter is active
header.bulkUnavailableDisabled: Unavailable while Disabled filter is active

toast.revealFailed: Could not reveal that mod with the current filters
toast.modsFolderNotConfigured: Mods folder is not configured yet

separatorDialog.createDescription: Create a library divider for custom order. You can drag mods into it later or use Move to Separator from the selection bar.
separatorDialog.renameDescription: Update the label shown for this separator in Custom Order.

conflict.redundantAria: Redundant: every deployed file is overwritten.

update.newVersion: New version on Nexus. Click to update.

delete.modDescription: You are about to permanently delete {name} from your mod library.
delete.separatorDescription: You are about to permanently delete the separator {name} from your mod library.
delete.allDescription: This permanently deletes every visible library entry, including separators. Enabled mods are removed from the game first, then erased from the library itself.
delete.selectedDescription: This permanently deletes every selected mod from the current library. Enabled mods are removed from the game first, then erased from disk.

detail.treeModeDeployed: Showing the last known deployment structure relative to the game root.
detail.treeModeInferred: Showing the inferred deployment structure based on indexed files and mod type.
detail.winsEmpty: This mod is not currently overwriting files from other mods.
detail.lossEmpty: No other mod is currently overwriting files from this mod.
detail.dialogRenameEntryDescription: Choose the new name for this entry inside the mod package.
detail.dialogCreateFolderDescription: Create a new folder in the currently targeted location of this mod.
detail.dialogRenameModDescription: Update the label shown for this mod in the library.
detail.dialogDeleteEntryDescription: This will permanently remove the selected file or folder from the mod package.
detail.toastSelectFirst: Select a file or folder first
detail.toastNoExactSource: This inferred folder has no exact source location in the mod package
detail.toastCannotRename: This entry cannot be renamed from the deploy tree view
detail.toastCannotDelete: This entry cannot be deleted from the deploy tree view

installBehavior.title: Install Behavior
installBehavior.description: Default behavior for archives that finish downloading through Nexus links.
installBehavior.toggleDescription: Nexus downloads install automatically when the archive is ready. Disable this if you prefer staging files in Downloads first.

runtimeCaptures.title: Runtime Captures
runtimeCaptures.description: Files written by mod tools (CET, RED4ext plugins) during gameplay are captured here and kept out of the game folder between sessions.
runtimeCaptures.clean: No captured files - game folder is clean.

language.title: Language
language.description: Choose the language Hyperion's interface is displayed in.

header.title: Settings
header.subtitle: Configure paths, Nexus access, and Hyperion updates without leaving the manager.

game.title: Game Path
game.description: Cyberpunk 2077 installation root used for launch validation and deployment.
game.placeholder: No folder selected - detect or browse
game.valid: Cyberpunk 2077 found. Launch and deployment validation are ready.
game.invalid: We couldn't find Cyberpunk 2077 in this folder. Launch stays blocked until it's fixed.

library.title: Mod Library
library.description: Managed archive repository for metadata, reinstalls, staged recovery, and deploy rebuilds.
library.placeholder: No folder selected - use suggested or browse
library.valid: Installs, rescans, and recovery can use this library.
library.invalid: This folder can't be used as a mod library. Installs stay blocked until it's fixed.

downloads.title: Downloads Intake
downloads.description: Optional source folder for incoming archives before install.
downloads.defaultDescription: Using the default folder next to your library.
downloads.activeDescription: New mod archives are picked up from here.

connection.title: Nexus Connection
connection.description: Paste your personal Nexus API key. Hyperion saves it automatically and validates it in the background.
connection.placeholder: Paste your Nexus API key here...

account.title: Account
account.description: Linked Nexus identity and subscription tier.
account.empty: Add a valid API key to show your account and unlock Nexus-aware flows.

free.feat1: Mod updates open Nexus in the browser
free.feat2: Downloads via nxm:// link from the site

premium.feat1: Mod updates download directly, no browser
premium.feat2: Direct CDN links resolved via Nexus API
premium.feat3: One-click inline install, stays in the Library

downloadFlow.title: Download Flow
downloadFlow.bullet1: Nexus links land in Downloads and install automatically unless disabled in General.
downloadFlow.bullet2: Hyperion still asks when a duplicate, FOMOD, or overwrite decision needs your input.

updates.title: Application Updates
updates.description: Check for, download, and install new Hyperion builds.

message.upToDate: Current build is up to date.

about.title: About Hyperion
about.description: Cyberpunk 2077 mod manager focused on virtual deployment, conflict visibility, and a predictable load order.
about.projectBody: Built for a clean Cyberpunk 2077 modding workflow: install, inspect, reorder, and launch without turning the game folder into the source of truth.
about.supportBody: For bug reports, include your Hyperion version and diagnostics summary so launch, VFS, and path issues are easier to trace.
about.creditsDescription: Acknowledgements for the tools, APIs, and modding references that make Hyperion possible.

credits.coreVfsBody: Hyperion's virtual deployment is powered by usvfs, the User-Space VFS used by Mod Organizer 2.
credits.serviceApiBody: Account validation, update checks, nxm links, and Premium-aware download handling.
credits.referenceBody: Archive behavior, load-order details, and public Cyberpunk modding documentation.

about.disclaimer: Hyperion is not affiliated with CD PROJEKT RED, Nexus Mods, Mod Organizer 2, or the usvfs maintainers.

toast.purged_other: Purged {count} active mods from the previous deployment
toast.purgeFailed_one: Could not fully purge {count} mod from the previous deployment
toast.purgeFailed_other: Could not fully purge {count} mods from the previous deployment
toast.restoreFailed_one: Loaded library, but {count} active mod could not be restored
toast.restoreFailed_other: Loaded library, but {count} active mods could not be restored
